today_trade_activity.admit_exit_pattern_detail: CHENNPETRO admit 09:42:35 → exit 10:44:50 (62 min, marginally outside strict 60-min window)

findings: Book is in a healthy state at 13:00: realized P&L +₹1,945.50 (+0.195% on capital), 7 diversified holdings spanning 5 sectors (BFSI, Pharma, Energy, Gold ETF, Shipping on full fill), no single position above 7.89% (PNBHOUSING) — well below the 15% max_position_pct cap with 7.11 pp buffer.

Capital deployment is low (36.39% filled / 45.76% on full fill of 2 OPEN orders), with cash at 63.55% — well above the 20% min cash buffer, and 19.24 pp BELOW the 65% max_deployed_pct cap. The cap is non-binding by a wide margin; under-deployment is signal-gated, not parameter-gated. Loosening max_deployed_pct would not unlock any additional admission since the current cap is not binding.

Sector concentration finding: BFSI re-stacked from 12.93% (this morning) to 17.63% on full-fill via the 12:12 BAJFINANCE BUY admission (3-name structure: PNBHOUSING 7.89% + BANDHANBNK 5.01% + BAJFINANCE 4.73%). This was the trigger for the 12:30 max_sector_pct revocation (0.28 → 0.22). At 13:00 the cap holds with 4.37 pp of buffer — binding by design but not breached. The 3-name BFSI shape morphologically echoes the Day-1/Day-2 originating pattern (AUBANK 18.6% + MAHABANK 9.78% + PNBHOUSING 7.87% = 36.27%) at roughly half the magnitude.

Today's strategy behavior is ACTIVE and BALANCED: 4 BUYs (ONGC, CHENNPETRO, GESHIP, BAJFINANCE) + 5 SELLs (POWERGRID, GLENMARK, TATAPOWER, CHENNPETRO, NESTLEIND) = 9 transactions across 4 hours, BUY:SELL ratio 0.80 (net SELL day). Notable: NESTLEIND exit at 11:12 captured +₹2,298.40 (largest single-trade win of experiment); FMCG 12.07% → 0%, freeing ₹122,398 cash. Strategy redeployed ₹93,460 of that into GESHIP (Shipping, +1 sector) + BAJFINANCE (BFSI add, concentration negative) — mixed signal of diversification + sector stacking simultaneously.

Confidence floor (0.65) is correctly admitting top-tier signal: ONGC at 0.72 was the FIRST high-confidence admission of the experiment (per 10:00 changelog). Today's other BUYs cleared at 0.66 (BAJFINANCE), 0.65 (GESHIP), 0.68 (CHENNPETRO) — all near floor. By the prompt's criterion ('high-confidence ≥0.70 entries being blocked'), the floor is NOT eating top-tier signal. Closed-trade sample n=12 today (4W/8L per cumulative tally with today's adds: GLENMARK W, TATAPOWER L, CHENNPETRO L, NESTLEIND W) — still 3 trades short of the n≥15 statistical threshold for confidence-statistical loosening (deferred to Day-10 milestone ~2026-05-04).

SL-band overrides bind correctly today: pre-tighten admissions CHENNPETRO 4.02% (admit 09:42 BEFORE 10:00 max_sl_pct 0.04→0.035 fire) and ONGC 3.97% are grandfathered; post-tighten admissions GESHIP 3.37% and BAJFINANCE 3.07% landed inside the new 3.5% cap. The cap is now demonstrably binding without breach.

NEW FINDING (observability only, no action this tick): BAJFINANCE was admitted with R:R = (985-944)/(944-915) = 41/29 = 1.4138, BELOW the base min_risk_reward floor of 1.5. This is a fresh warn-not-block instance — the engine admitted despite sub-floor R:R, mirroring the GLENMARK 4.4% SL / CHENNPETRO 4.02% SL pattern of engine warning rather than blocking near the threshold. Logged as n=1 to seed an n=2 trigger for the 13:30 risk monitor: a 2nd sub-1.5 R:R admission would clear the evidence bar to seed a new min_risk_reward override.

No pre-armed trigger from the 12:30 spec fires at 13:00: BFSI count holds at 3 names (no 4th cluster-emergence); BFSI 17.63% < 22% threshold; Pharma at 2 names / 8.77% (no 3rd name, well below 14% threshold); no 3rd ≥3.5% SL admission today; admit-then-exit pattern n=1 (and CHENNPETRO's 62-min cycle is marginally outside the strict 60-min window); composite distress meta-trigger fires 0/3 (positive book, no single-name distress, strategy active).

No clean LOOSEN path fires either: max_deployed_pct 0.65→0.70 needs ≥2 high-conf entries today AND >55% deployment — currently 1/2 high-conf + 45.76% deployment (9.24 pp under threshold), both legs fail; min_confidence 0.65→0.60 deferred to Day-10 for n≥15 sample; max_sector_pct 0.22→0.28 requires BFSI<13% sustained (BFSI just rose to 17.63%, opposite direction). Loosening against an active sector-stacking pattern would compound the wrong direction.

Cumulative override stack is heavy (7 active overrides representing ~50.5 pp of notional total tightening: min_confidence +15pp, max_position_pct -5pp, max_deployed_pct -15pp, default_sl_pct -0.5pp, min_sl_pct +0.5pp, max_sl_pct -1.5pp, max_sector_pct -13pp). Per the prompt's cost doctrine, every notch loses some legitimate signal — disciplined HOLD avoids reflexive re-tightening when no trigger fires.
